description: Intersection lateral-crossing truck from the left on a constructed collision course with the ego. The nominal right-escape corridor is made unsafe by a pedestrian on the right plus a right-side blockage, and a following vehicle is present to contest hard braking.
hypothesis: When a left-side crossing threat would normally favor a right drift, a pedestrian/blocked corridor on the right should suppress the drift; the boundary should shift between hard braking and a left/behind-threat evasive path as TTC, ego speed, lateral closing speed, and follower gap tighten.
